text.title: Conversion Metrics, ICE Scoring, and Event Tracking
text.summary: Key metric definitions with good/great thresholds, ICE prioritization tiers, and the 6 standard funnel analytics events to implement.

list.item: Overall conversion: Final step / First step. Good: 1-3%, Great: 3-5%+
Step conversion: Next step / This step. Good: 60-80%, Great: 80-90%
Drop-off rate: (This - Next) / This. Good: 10-40%, Great: below 10%
Time to convert: Median days from first touch to conversion. Good: under 7 days, Great: under 1 day

section.title: ICE Scoring

rule.statement: Score every hypothesis with ICE = Impact x Confidence x Ease before adding it to the test queue. Run scores 20-30 immediately; scores below 10 go to backlog.
rule.rationale: Without scoring, teams default to easiest tests, not highest-impact tests. ICE makes the opportunity cost of each choice explicit.

section.reference: ICE scoring worksheet with example scores

section.title: Analytics Event Tracking

rule.statement: Instrument all 6 standard funnel events before running any optimization. Missing events make drop-off diagnosis impossible.
rule.rationale: You cannot optimize steps you cannot measure.

section.reference: Analytics event tracking implementation for all 6 funnel steps

example.note: Track Homepage Viewed (with trafficSource, device), CTA Clicked (with ctaLocation, device), Signup Form Viewed, Signup Completed (with method, planSelected), Onboarding Started (with flow type), First Action Completed (with timeToAction).

section.reference: A/B test spec template for documenting control, variant, metrics, and decision criteria
